perturbation.original_text: Aimmune shall conduct the Development Activities in accordance with sound and ethical business and scientific practices, and in compliance with all Applicable Law, including GCPs and GLPs, and also including all applicable data privacy and data protection laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Aimmune shall endeavor to conduct the Development Activities in accordance with what it considers to be sound and ethical business and scientific practices, and will aim to be in compliance with Applicable Law, including GCPs and GLPs, and also including all applicable data privacy and data protection laws.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates compliance with all Applicable Laws, GCPs, and GLPs. The modified text introduces ambiguity by replacing 'shall conduct' with 'shall endeavor to conduct' and 'in compliance' with 'will aim to be in compliance.' This suggests that Aimmune only needs to make an effort to comply, rather than fully complying, weakening the legal obligation. This contradicts the strict requirements of GCP (Good Clinical Practice) regulations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 21 CFR Part 312 - Investigational New Drug Application
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 312.20 outlines the requirements for an investigational new drug application, including ensuring that clinical investigations are conducted according to strict scientific standards. By weakening the obligation to 'aim to be in compliance' rather than 'in compliance', the modified text contradicts the FDA's mandate for rigorous adherence to regulatory standards in clinical trials.

perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'shall conduct' with 'shall endeavor to conduct' and 'in compliance' with 'will aim to be in compliance,' thus weakening the obligation to adhere to Applicable Laws, GCPs, and GLPs. The original text mandates full compliance, while the revised text suggests that Aimmune only needs to make an effort to comply. According to the law explanation, 21 CFR § 312.20 requires clinical investigations to be conducted according to strict scientific standards. The changed text undermines this strict adherence by introducing ambiguity, making it only an aim rather than a requirement to be in compliance.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party will also use its Commercially Reasonable Efforts to require any agents or independent contractors performing an activity pursuant to this Agreement to assign all Inventions that are developed, made or conceived by such agents or independent contractors to the relevant Party, according to the ownership principles described in Section 9.1.1 free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, charges, security interests, mortgages or other similar restrictions.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will use its best efforts, if commercially reasonable, to encourage any agents or independent contractors performing an activity pursuant to this Agreement to assign all Inventions that are developed, made or conceived by such agents or independent contractors to the relevant Party, according to the ownership principles described in Section 9.1.1 free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, charges, security interests, mortgages or other similar restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the use of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to require assignment of inventions. The modified text weakens this by using 'best efforts, if commercially reasonable, to encourage' which makes the assignment discretionary rather than obligatory. This contradicts standard intellectual property law practices where employers typically secure assignment of inventions from contractors.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Labor Code Section 2870 addresses situations where an employer requires an employee (or by extension, a contractor) to assign their inventions. While it protects employees from assigning inventions made on their own time without using employer resources, it implies that employers generally have a right to assignment for work-related inventions. By making the assignment discretionary and only encouraged, the modified text could be interpreted as a waiver of rights to inventions conceived during contracted work, weakening intellectual property protection for Xencor/Aimmune, which is in contradiction to common interpretations of CA Labor Code 2870
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, LAB 2870 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Labor Code - LAB DIVISION 3. EMPLOYMENT RELATIONS [2700 - 3122.4] ( Division 3 enacted by Stats. 1937, Ch. 90. ) CHAPTER 2. Employer and Employee [2750 - 2930] ( Chapter 2 enacted by Stats. 1937, Ch. 90. ) ARTICLE 3.5. Inventions Made by an Employee [2870 - 2872] ( Article 3.5 added by Stats. 1979, Ch. 1001. ) 2870. (a) Any provision in an employment agreement which provides that an employee shall assign, or offer to assign, any of his or her rights in an invention to his or her employer shall not apply to an invention that the employee developed entirely on his or her own time without using the employer’s equipment, supplies, facilities, or trade secret information except for those inventions that either: (1) Relate at the time of conception or reduction to practice of the invention to the employer’s business, or actual or demonstrably anticipated research or development of the employer; or (2) Result from any work performed by the employee for the employer. (b) To the extent a provision in an employment agreement purports to require an employee to assign an invention otherwise excluded from being required to be assigned under subdivision (a), the provision is against the public policy of this state and is unenforceable. (Amended by Stats. 1991, Ch. 647, Sec. 5.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to assign inventions from contractors. The original text states each party 'will use its Commercially Reasonable Efforts to require' assignment, whereas the changed text states each party 'will use its best efforts, if commercially reasonable, to encourage.' This shift makes the assignment discretionary, weakening the intellectual property protection for the company. The LLM Law Explanation references California Labor Code Section 2870, implying that employers generally have a right to assignment for work-related inventions, which is weakened by the changed contract. While snippet 1 addresses employee inventions, the Law Explanation extends this to contractors as well. The modification weakens the likelihood of assignment of inventions and creates an ambiguous legal obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Aimmune shall keep Xencor informed as to its progress and activities relating to the Development, Manufacture and Commercialization of the Product on [***] basis (i.e., every [***] ([***]) months), including by providing updates on the status of studies necessary for obtaining Regulatory Approval with respect to the Product, regulatory matters and meetings with Regulatory Authorities with respect to the Product, and Commercialization activities commencing no later than [***] ([***]) year prior to the date on which Aimmune estimates the First Commercial Sale of Product will occur.
perturbation.changed_text: Aimmune will use reasonable efforts to keep Xencor generally informed as to its high-level progress and activities relating to the Development, Manufacture and Commercialization of the Product on an occasional basis, including by providing limited updates, if any, on the status of key studies necessary for obtaining Regulatory Approval with respect to the Product, major regulatory matters and meetings with Regulatory Authorities with respect to the Product, and Commercialization activities commencing no later than a few months prior to the date on which Aimmune anticipates the First Commercial Sale of Product may occur.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that Aimmune 'shall keep Xencor informed' with specified frequency. The modified text changes this to 'will use reasonable efforts to keep Xencor generally informed on an occasional basis' introducing ambiguity and weakening the obligation, the 'high-level' qualifier reduces the amount of information that needs to be provided and 'limited updates, if any' further exacerbates the weakened requirement. Additionally, 'anticipates the First Commercial Sale may occur' reduces reliability and increases ambiguity. This contradicts the requirements of ongoing communication critical for pharmacovigilance as mandated by FDA regulations.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 314.81 specifies postmarketing reporting requirements for drug products, including adverse drug experiences. It mandates ongoing monitoring and communication of safety information to the FDA. By weakening Aimmune's obligation to keep Xencor informed, the modified text hinders Xencor's ability to monitor the safety profile of the Product effectively and report potential adverse events in a timely manner, potentially leading to non-compliance with FDA's postmarketing surveillance requirements.

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens Aimmune's obligation to keep Xencor informed, shifting from 'shall keep Xencor informed' to 'will use reasonable efforts to keep Xencor generally informed on an occasional basis'. This introduces ambiguity and weakens the original requirement. The law explanation states that 21 CFR § 314.81 specifies postmarketing reporting requirements, mandating ongoing monitoring and communication of safety information. The scraped snippet provides context for 21 CFR 314.81, reinforcing the existence of these regulations. By reducing the frequency and depth of information shared, the modified text potentially hinders Xencor's ability to comply with postmarketing surveillance requirements, creating a contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities Legal Contradiction' definition.
